column11:
I work for NYC?no raises since 2019 until this year. Currently I make $114,000. The $128,081 pay reflects 3% retroactive increases coming this April (for 2021, 2022, 2023), and another 3% coming in May 2024. There is also a one-time-only $3k for retention coming in late April. But it?s more like a bonus as it can?t be used to adjust our overall pay the way the 3% increases can. 
There will be another 3% raise coming in May 2025. Then most likely no additional raises for a number of years. 

We are non-union lines and so the union negotiations come first and we follow between 6-18 months behind them. 

We also get a pension with big jumps in benefits at 5, 10, and 20 years of service. At 10 years we can retire from the city and get employee-type medical benefits (for self and family). Once one is Medicare-eligible , the city promised to pay co-pays and drug costs for Medicare for self and family, but unions and mayor are trying to push us all into MAP plan ( which many doctors outside of NYC?and more and more within NYC?are refusing to take). Retirees have won 4/4 lawsuits to prevent this. Latest one was argued in front of the NYC court of appeals last week?judgement is pending. You can read more about it at https://www.nycretirees.org/healthcare and    https://www.nycretirees.org/announcements

column6: More like "director" - I manage all social media and marketing for 3x luxury dealerships (same brand). We are part of a dealership group with 60 total dealerships. I work closely with our 3rd party digital marketing team, although they take credit for a lot of my work. In reality, I do about 85% of their work, too, including mockups, sourcing photos, language, etc. If it has our name on it, it's either come through me or been created by me. Each dealership has its own social media "voice" (that is 100% me)
column11:
Our company does not allow you to be salaried unless you are an actual "manager" with a team to oversee. So, even though my boss views me as a manager and treats me as such, I am technically an hourly employee. I make $33.50/hour. Some weeks are a little less than 40, some a little more. As we know, social media doesn't "sleep" - so generally speaking, if the dealership is open, our social media is some level of active. My boss is working on a new additional bonus structure for something he's asked me to oversee. I haven't signed off on it yet, but he said it will be retro active once he gets all the documentation sorted. The additional income won't be too much - 10% of a particular sales metric, paid quarterly. Obviously, the more we sell, the more I'll make, but for Q1 2024, it's about $50.

Also: I recognize that I'm probably UNDER paid for my level of responsibility. The auto industry is way behind the times. This is a sacrifice I'm willing to make because of the level of freedom and support I'm given in all work areas/functions.
